top10.introHtml: Top 10 best buys for a cheap fun second car near Media, PA — ranked by reliability, convertible-top condition indicators, model pedigree, and value. Condition beats miles on this list.

picks.whyGood: The MX-5 Miata is the benchmark cheap fun convertible — 50/50 weight, rev-happy 2.0L, bulletproof drivetrain that laughs at 200k miles. The Grand Touring adds leather, heated seats, and Bose audio. At $11,450 with 177k on the clock, this is well into Miata's 'second life' zone where depreciation is done and the engine is properly broken in. Mazda's NA/NB/NC family has one of the strongest DIY communities on the planet.
picks.whatToVerify: Timing chain (chain, not belt on NC — no service interval needed). Pull the oil cap and check for mayo; look for head-gasket weep at the back of the block. Soft-top: operate through full cycle, check rear plastic window clarity and seam condition. Inspect door sill rust (common on NC under carpet). Test limited-slip diff if equipped. Ask for service history.
picks.bottomLine: The definitive answer to 'cheap fun convertible' — buy it.

picks.whyGood: The Z3 roadster (1996–2002) is the most driver-focused bargain BMW makes. The 2.3L and 2.5i engines are robust to 200k with regular oil changes. Manual top opens in seconds. This one checks in under $8k with clean-title/no-accident status — genuinely rare for a Z3 at this price.
picks.whatToVerify: Oil leaks from valve cover, VANOS solenoids (rough idle), power steering rack leaks. Soft-top: run through full cycle, check headliner condition. Check for rust on trunk lid lower seam and door bottoms. Cooling system — Z3s eat coolant hoses with age; budget $400 for a full hose refresh if not done.
picks.bottomLine: Best Z3 deal in the search — snappy handling and sub-$8k entry price.

picks.whyGood: The Solstice is GM's closest answer to the Miata — rear-wheel drive, 2.4L Ecotec (177 hp), Kappa platform. Under 35k miles makes it nearly pristine for the age. The base Ecotec is more reliable than the turbocharged GXP variant. Manual or automatic, both are fine.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the soft-top through a full cycle; the Solstice top is simple but seals degrade. Check for cabin water intrusion (soggy carpet behind seats). Inspect the rear-window seam. Oil leaks from cam cover are common. Test A/C.
picks.bottomLine: Low-mileage Solstice at this price is a rare find — reliable and genuinely fun.

picks.whyGood: The E46 330Ci (2001–2006) is one of BMW's finest driver's cars — inline-6, precise steering, proper rear-wheel drive. Under 90k miles with no accidents and a convertible top that still seals is increasingly hard to find at this price.
picks.whatToVerify: E46: check cooling system (plastic thermostat housing cracks), rear subframe reinforcement mounts (critical — inspect or walk away), convertible top hydraulic rams (slow or leaky = $400 repair), front control arm bushings. Pull CARFAX. Convertible top: full cycle test including tonneau cover.
picks.bottomLine: One of the best driving experiences under $15k — do the pre-purchase inspection.

picks.whyGood: The MR2 Spyder (2000–2005) is the most driver-focused car on this entire list — mid-engine, 6-speed, sub-2,400 lb curb weight. Toyota reliability means 74k miles is early in this engine's life. Rarely floods the used market under $15k; finding one locally is a genuine opportunity.
picks.whatToVerify: Mid-engine layout means any oil or coolant leaks need immediate attention — access is tight. Check throttle body (TPS issues on high-mileage examples). Soft-top: inspect rear plastic window. Test SMT paddle shift if applicable. No AWD or heavy features to worry about — it's elegantly simple.
picks.bottomLine: The most driver-focused car in this search — if the top and engine check out, buy it immediately.

picks.whyGood: The Solstice GXP adds a 260-hp Ecotec turbo to the Kappa platform — 0-60 in 5.5s, still under $11k. At 51k miles this is well sorted. Manual gearbox option makes this a proper performance convertible.
picks.whatToVerify: GXP turbo: check intercooler hoses and BOV for cracks, inspect for oil consumption. Soft top seals, rear window condition. Budget for upgraded intercooler if tracking. Ensure the tune hasn't been pushed beyond factory limits.
picks.bottomLine: Same fun platform as the base Solstice but with a proper performance punch at a fair price.

picks.whyGood: The Crossfire Limited uses the Mercedes SLK platform under the hood — supercharged 3.2L V6, rear-wheel drive, Benz hardware at Chrysler prices. The Limited adds a Roadster roof. At $10,987 with 64k miles and no accidents 2 miles from Media PA, the price is right.
picks.whatToVerify: SLK platform: top motor is the Achilles heel — operate through full cycle. Check for Crossfire-specific top seam failure at rear. The Mercedes-sourced drivetrain is generally solid but repair costs are Mercedes-priced. Supercharger belt condition.
picks.bottomLine: Mercedes-based muscle at Chrysler money — top condition is everything, so verify it carefully.

picks.whyGood: A clean S197 Mustang V6 convertible under $15k is a practical fun car — 305 hp, available manual, solid Mustang dealer network, easy parts. The V6 has proven reliable to 200k+ miles. This one comes with a Great Deal rating.
picks.whatToVerify: S197 top: operate full cycle, check rear seat for water staining from seal failure. Rear axle clunk on takeoff (common — 8.8 pinion bearing). T-3650 third-gear synchro (shift slowly into 3rd). Inspect convertible-top mounting points at rocker for rust.
picks.bottomLine: Practical, easy-to-maintain fun car with strong parts support — the safe choice.

picks.whyGood: The E85 Z4 (2003–2008) with 2.5i engine is a refined roadster — silky inline-6, better interior than the Z3, and now settling into deep depreciation. The 2.5i at 88k miles with no accidents is in the reliable zone.
picks.whatToVerify: Z4 2.5i: cooling system (same plastic hose aging as Z3), VANOS units, oil leaks from valve cover and oil filter housing. Manual top: operate full cycle. Check for frame damage around hood hinges (these fold back and get abused). Soft-top hydraulics if power-top equipped.
picks.bottomLine: A more refined roadster than the Z3 at a similar price — inspect the cooling system first.

picks.whyGood: The Saturn Sky shares the Kappa platform with the Solstice — same rear-drive fun, slightly sharper styling, and parts-bin commonality with the entire GM family. The Roadster trim at 30k miles is exceptional — many of these sat in collections after GM's Saturn brand ended.
picks.whatToVerify: Same checks as Solstice: soft-top cycle test, rear window seam, cabin water intrusion, cam cover oil leaks. GM Ecotec engine is robust. Parts availability: some Saturn-specific pieces are harder to find but Solstice parts cross-reference widely.
picks.bottomLine: Same platform as the Solstice with rarer, sharper styling — excellent value at this mileage.

mustangs.introHtml: All Ford Mustang convertibles in the search area, ranked by trim level and value. GT trim (V8) scores highest; V6 models offer the same open-air fun at lower cost.

picks.whyGood: 1994 Mustang GT — 44,000 miles at $10,500. GT trim means the 4.6L or 5.0L V8 — the whole point of the Mustang.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate convertible top through full cycle and check rear-seat water staining from top seal failure. Inspect rocker panels at convertible-top mount points for rust (SN-95 especially). Test T-3650 third-gear synchro by slow-shifting into 3rd. Check 8.8 rear axle for clunk on takeoff. Inspect all top seals.

picks.whyGood: 2014 Mustang V6 — 61,109 miles at $14,294. V6 is the value choice — reliable 3.7L, lower insurance, same top-down fun.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate convertible top through full cycle and check rear-seat water staining from top seal failure. Inspect rocker panels at convertible-top mount points for rust (SN-95 especially). Test T-3650 third-gear synchro by slow-shifting into 3rd. Check 8.8 rear axle for clunk on takeoff. Inspect all top seals.

picks.whyGood: 2014 Mustang  — 61,109 miles at $14,594. V6 is the value choice — reliable 3.7L, lower insurance, same top-down fun.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate convertible top through full cycle and check rear-seat water staining from top seal failure. Inspect rocker panels at convertible-top mount points for rust (SN-95 especially). Test T-3650 third-gear synchro by slow-shifting into 3rd. Check 8.8 rear axle for clunk on takeoff. Inspect all top seals.

picks.whyGood: 2013 Mustang V6 — 64,381 miles at $10,499. V6 is the value choice — reliable 3.7L, lower insurance, same top-down fun.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate convertible top through full cycle and check rear-seat water staining from top seal failure. Inspect rocker panels at convertible-top mount points for rust (SN-95 especially). Test T-3650 third-gear synchro by slow-shifting into 3rd. Check 8.8 rear axle for clunk on takeoff. Inspect all top seals.

picks.whyGood: 2014 Mustang Convertible — 61,000 miles at $13,495. V6 is the value choice — reliable 3.7L, lower insurance, same top-down fun.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate convertible top through full cycle and check rear-seat water staining from top seal failure. Inspect rocker panels at convertible-top mount points for rust (SN-95 especially). Test T-3650 third-gear synchro by slow-shifting into 3rd. Check 8.8 rear axle for clunk on takeoff. Inspect all top seals.
